what_it_does: Defines a standing adversarial routine (the COMPLETION-REFUTER / popcorn-B) that periodically scans company channel and board completion claims, reads the criteria and channel history, attacks each claim from a default-to-wrong stance, and posts the resulting holes to the lead.
observations: The file is a ROUTINE dictionary with metadata (id, label, description, prompt, cwd, permission_mode, cadence, repeats, max_turns, trigger) rather than imperative code.
The docstring frames the routine as Tim's 2026-06-21 directive after an over-claimed 'complete' status.

The prompt instructs reading active channels via mcp__company__cc_channel, criteria files (OPERATOR-LOOP-CLOSURE.md, COMMISSION-COMPLETION-REGISTER.md, CHANNEL-LOOP-BOARD.md), and attacking every 'complete/done/live/verified/wired' claim.
It distinguishes 'pieces verified by the fabric' from 'the closed loop verified by Tim's actual end-to-end use' and treats the former as not done.
It targets the fabric's own completion claims, including the lead's, to counter confirmation bias.
Cadence is 'every:1800' (30 minutes), repeats=True, max_turns=12, permission_mode='default'.
Output is broadcast/replied to the channel addressed to the lead id 'ch-3mpkjg3r'.
It references the popcorn adversarial kernel and notes a future generalized composable version will supersede this hard-coded one.
standouts: Default-to-wrong law: the routine must try hard to break every completion claim before conceding it survives.
Completion bar is Tim's end-to-end operator-loop use, not fabric self-verification of pieces.
Scheduled every 30 minutes plus fire-on-demand when completion language appears.
Explicitly addresses the lead (ch-3mpkjg3r) with a structured [claim] → [hole] → [evidence] list.
conventions: Follows the routine schema: id, label, description, prompt, cwd, permission_mode, cadence, repeats, max_turns, trigger.
Uses a long, imperative natural-language prompt to drive an LLM-based routine runner.
References external markdown criteria files by relative path inside the prompt.
Uses mcp__company__cc_channel operations for channel I/O.
concerns: Hard-coded lead channel id 'ch-3mpkjg3r' and criteria file paths make the routine brittle to renames or multi-lead setups.
No executable logic or error handling in the file; correctness depends entirely on the routine runner interpreting the prompt.
Permission_mode 'default' is vague about what reads/posts are actually allowed.
max_turns=12 may be tight for reading history, criteria, live verification, and posting holes in one run.
The prompt could produce high-volume, repetitive channel noise if it fires on every completion claim.
No mechanism shown for deduplicating previously reported holes or tracking claim resolution.
notes: A scheduled adversarial audit routine that treats every 'done' claim as guilty until proven closed-loop by Tim's actual use.
questions: How does the routine runner parse this dict and bind the prompt to tool calls?
Where is the generalized composable popcorn-B version that will supersede this hard-coded routine?
How are live verification steps (curl, grep) executed safely by the LLM runner?
How is the lead channel id 'ch-3mpkjg3r' assigned and kept in sync?
What prevents duplicate hole reports for the same claim across the 30-minute cadence?
What is the exact semantics of permission_mode 'default' in this runner?
How is 'fire-on-demand' triggered when completion language appears?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file defines a routine whose prompt exactly performs the stated adversarial completion-refutation against channel claims and criteria.
apparent_themes: Closed-loop, user-verified completion over piecewise fabric self-verification.
Accountability and escalation to a human lead.
Operational loop closure as the single bar for 'done'.
intention_signals: It tries to institutionalize persistent skepticism inside the fabric so that 'complete' cannot be self-certified by the system; only Tim's end-to-end use earns the label, and gaps are surfaced to the lead immediately.
novelty: A standing, timer-driven adversarial refutation routine embedded in the company's own routine runner, explicitly targeting the fabric's own confirmation bias and completion language.
contribution: Provides a concrete routine schema and adversarial prompt pattern that a fused system can reuse to detect premature completion claims, distinguish piecewise progress from closed-loop delivery, and route unresolved gaps to the responsible lead.
summary_for_embedding: A routine definition that configures a scheduled adversarial checker to read channel completion claims and criteria, attack each claim from a default-to-wrong stance, and post evidence-backed holes to the lead.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent the fabric from self-certifying 'done' by forcing every completion claim to survive scrutiny against Tim's end-to-end operator-loop use.
